title: Habenaria ituriensis

intro: Habenaria ituriensis, also known as the Ituri Habenaria, is a species of orchid native to the Ituri Rainforest in the Democratic Republic of Congo. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and malaria, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Habenaria ituriensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
